description: Every skill has a target — the entity that receives the effect. If a skill does not explicitly define a target_resolver, the default target is the caster itself (self).
rule: target_resolver is OPTIONAL. If omitted, target = caster. If defined, it specifies how the engine resolves who receives the effect.

self.description: The caster receives the effect. Used by movement skills (walk, jump), buffs, self-heals.
self.note: This is the DEFAULT. Skills like walk and jump don't need to declare it — they affect the caster by definition.

area.description: Whatever enters the trigger area receives the effect. Used by hit, AoE attacks, zones.
area.resolution: The trigger (Area3D) detects overlaps. Each overlapping body is a target.
area.note: The hit skill spawns a trigger cube. Any CharacterBody3D that overlaps it gets pushed.

single.description: A single specific target. Used by ranged attacks, lock-on skills.
single.resolution: Raycast or lock-on system picks one target.

all_enemies.description: All enemies in range. Used by AoE ultimates.
all_enemies.resolution: Query all bodies in radius, filter by team/faction.

walk.explanation: No target_resolver defined. Default = self. The caster moves themselves.

jump.explanation: No target_resolver defined. Default = self. The caster jumps.

hit.explanation: Target = whoever activates the trigger (enters the hitbox cube). Could be an enemy, a destructible, anything with a collision body.
